claim: The H3.3K36me3 histone reader protein ZMYND11 has a  higher binding affinity if G34R/V is mutated.

result.claim: The H3.3K36me3 histone reader protein ZMYND11 has a  higher binding affinity if G34R/V is mutated.
result.reasoning: A thorough search of the knowledge base using various combinations of keywords such as "ZMYND11 binding affinity G34R/V mutation H3.3K36me3", "ZMYND11 H3.3K36me3 binding", "G34R/V mutation histone binding", and "ZMYND11 G34R/V mutation" did not yield any direct evidence to support or refute the claim. The search results primarily focused on other unrelated mutations, genes, or general binding mechanisms, indicating a lack of information specifically addressing the binding affinity of ZMYND11 to H3.3K36me3 when G34R/V is mutated.

evidence.text: Mutational activation of PIK3CA commonly co-occurs with KRAS mutation.
evidence.source: PIK3CA mutation uncouples tumor growth and Cyclin D1 regulation from MEK/ERK and mutant KRAS signaling

evidence.text: In KRAS mutant tumors, PIK3CA mutation restores Cyclin D1 expression and G1/S cell cycle progression.
evidence.source: PIK3CA mutation uncouples tumor growth and Cyclin D1 regulation from MEK/ERK and mutant KRAS signaling

evidence.text: Polyglutamine binding protein 1 (PQBP1) emerged as a strong candidate in the analysis.
